 {"id":"bd-3984","title":"Conformance: nicobailon extensions (6 exts, npm packages)","description":"Conformance tests for nicobailon's popular npm-published Pi extensions. Extensions (6): 1. pi-mcp-adapter (npm) — Token-efficient MCP server integration, proxy tool, lazy loading 2. pi-subagents (npm) — Async subagent delegation with truncation and artifacts 3. pi-interactive-shell (npm v0.7.1) — Full PTY emulation overlay, no tmux, user takeover 4. pi-interview-tool — Web-based form tool with keyboard navigation 5. pi-powerline-footer — Powerline-style status bar with git integration 6. pi-rewind-hook — Rewind file changes with git-based checkpoints. The npm packages are particularly important as they test our package resolution pipeline. pi-mcp-adapter is one of the most popular community extensions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:19:07.555554504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:38:07.223191449Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:38:07.223013477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3984","depends_on_id":"bd-1oi7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:19:07.567626938Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39jj","title":"Conformance: Tier 1a — Tool-Only Extensions (7 exts)","description":"Conformance tests for the simplest extension category: single-file extensions that only register tools (no events, no commands, no UI). These test the core pi.registerTool() pathway.

Extensions (7):
1. hello.ts — Minimal greeting tool, returns text + details payload
2. tools.ts — Registers multiple tools demonstrating the full tool API
3. tool-override.ts — Overrides built-in tool behavior (wraps for logging/ACL)
4. truncated-tool.ts — Wraps ripgrep with output truncation
5. question.ts — Interactive question tool
6. questionnaire.ts — Multi-question questionnaire tool
7. qna.ts — Q&A extraction tool

For each extension:
- Load in QuickJS, verify registration succeeds
- Invoke each registered tool with sample parameters
- Assert output content matches reference (text, details, is_error)
- Assert tool schema (parameters JSON Schema) matches reference
- Test error cases (missing params, invalid input)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:15:45.253645023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:27:42.152775194Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:27:42.152577084Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39jj","depends_on_id":"bd-s2zr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:15:45.297232749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39q8","title":"Phase 2: Custom streamSimple handler for extension providers","description":"Add support for custom streaming handlers in extension-registered providers.

## Background
Phase 1 (bd-1oj9) implements basic provider registration with standard APIs.
This phase adds support for custom streaming via JavaScript callbacks.

## API Addition
```typescript
pi.registerProvider(name, {
  // ... base config
  streamSimple?: (request) => AsyncIterable<string>
});
```

## Implementation Requirements
1. Async callback bridge from Rust to JS
2. Stream chunks from JS handler to agent loop
3. Error handling for JS exceptions during streaming
4. Cancellation support (cancel JS stream on interrupt)

## Technical Challenges
- JS -> Rust async iteration
- Back-pressure handling
- Error propagation across language boundary

## Test Plan
- Unit test: custom streamSimple called for provider
- Integration test: streaming works end-to-end
- Test: cancellation stops JS stream

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (stream bridge)
- src/extensions_js.rs (async callback support)
- src/provider.rs (use extension stream)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:36:36.961318176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:30:01.456330680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:29:25.285844625Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39q8","depends_on_id":"bd-1oj9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:36:59.163581435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":247,"issue_id":"bd-39q8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. All 4 requirements implemented.","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:30:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-39u","title":"Unit tests: JS runtime + shims + event loop ordering","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires correct promise/microtask ordering and shim behavior.
- This bead is the **ordering/invariants test suite** for bd-123 + bd-1f5.

Steps:
- Unit/property tests for the PiJS event loop ordering:
  - macrotask vs microtask fixpoint draining
  - timer tie-breaking stability
  - hostcall completion enqueue order stability
- Tests for cancellation + timeout behavior across the promise bridge.
- Trace-log assertions for ordering and promise resolution sequence (diffable).

Acceptance:
- Ordering invariants from bd-123 hold and are reproducible under deterministic harness control.
- These tests protect the promise that the pinned sample set can be run with stable traces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

No new research or testing here; only publication of validated results.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:41.990925896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T21:51:04.270212461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a24","depends_on_id":"bd-205q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:22:08.935958765Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a24","depends_on_id":"bd-2fps","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:22:00.593426251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a24","depends_on_id":"bd-ofcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:41.998618579Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":323,"issue_id":"bd-3a24","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Once validated, the extension set must be communicated clearly to users and future maintainers.

 {"id":"bd-3am2","title":"Workstream: Error UX (actionable suggestions + context-aware hints)","description":"# Goal
Improve error UX so failures are not just "what happened" but also "what to do next" (actionable suggestions) and "why this happened here" (context-aware hints).

# Background
Pi runs in many environments (different terminals, missing system deps like `fd`, missing API keys, VCR playback mode, etc.). The port already has structured error types, but the user experience depends on:
- concise, readable rendering
- correct categorization
- a small set of high-signal remediation hints

# Principles
- Hints must be **actionable** (specific command/path/setting), not generic.
- Hints must be **non-spammy**: show at most a couple of suggestions per error.
- Hints must be **deterministic** and testable (unit/integration tests assert on messages).

# Candidate Hint Categories (examples)
- Missing API key → point to `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / etc and settings override.
- Missing system dependency (`fd`, `rg`) → exact install commands per OS.
- VCR cassette missing in CI → explain `VCR_MODE` + cassette dir + how to record.
- Permission denied reading/writing files → show the path and suggest chmod/ownership checks.
- Invalid config JSON → show file path + first parse error location.
- Provider auth failure (401) → suggest `/login` or `--api-key` usage.

# Deliverables
- A mapping from core error variants → short "next steps" hints.
- Rendering integration for:
  - interactive TUI error panels
  - print mode stderr
  - RPC mode error payloads (where appropriate)
- Tests validating the most important hint paths.

# Non-Goals
- Not building a full diagnostics engine.
- Not adding web browsing or auto-fixing.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:22:50.853156366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:30:10.750740972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T09:33:19.417964249Z","close_reason":"Workstream complete: hint taxonomy/mapping (bd-1iz5), rendering integration (bd-2kj0), and common-mode tests (bd-1ywr) all closed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3am2","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ap","title":"Implement TUI state transition tests","description":"# Implement TUI state transition tests

## Goal
Test the interactive TUI's state machine logic - how it responds to messages
and user input events.

## Background
PiApp in src/interactive.rs implements the bubbletea Model trait:
- init() - Initialize state
- update(msg) - Handle messages, return commands
- view() - Render current state

The update() function is the state machine - it needs thorough testing.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for each test.
- Log initial state, input message, and key state deltas.
- On failure, dump a compact state diff (selected fields) and the rendered view.

## Test Categories

### 1. Keyboard Input Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_escape_quits() {
    let mut app = create_app();
    let cmd = app.update(KeyMsg::Escape);
    assert!(matches!(cmd, Cmd::Quit));
}
```

### 2. Agent Event Tests
- Start/end events toggle streaming
- Text delta accumulation
- Tool call lifecycle updates

### 3. Slash Command Tests
- /help, /model, /thinking, /clear

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Error display and clear-on-input behavior

### 5. Viewport/Scrolling Tests
- Scroll to bottom on new output
- PageUp/PageDown behavior

## Dependencies
- bd-1d3 (Snapshot infrastructure for view testing)

## Files
- tests/tui_state.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 30+ state transition tests
- [ ] All key handlers tested
- [ ] All PiMsg variants tested
- [ ] All slash commands tested
- [ ] Error handling tested
- [ ] Scrolling tested
- [ ] Logs include inputs + state deltas
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:51.898944355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:26:16.070609065Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T10:01:45.273008082Z","close_reason":"Implemented 30+ TUI state transition tests in tests/tui_state.rs; full gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ap","depends_on_id":"bd-1d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ap","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ap","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
